metadata.summary: Implement Kubernetes canary deployments manually via replica-weight or Nginx Ingress annotations, and with Argo Rollouts for automated progressive delivery with Prometheus-based analysis.

content.title: Kubernetes Canary and Progressive Delivery
content.summary: Canary deployment gradually shifts traffic from a stable version to a new version, using metrics analysis to decide whether to promote or rollback. Manual canary uses replica-count weighting or Nginx Ingress annotations. Argo Rollouts (2025-2026 standard) automates step-wise traffic shifts with Prometheus metric analysis and automatic rollback on threshold breach.

why.p: A rolling update replaces all pods with no traffic control — a bad version reaches 100% of users before enough signal accumulates to trigger a rollback. Canary limits blast radius: 10% of traffic to the new version means 90% of users are unaffected if the new version has a bug. Progressive delivery adds automated analysis so humans need not watch dashboards through a 30-minute promotion window.

list.item: Production releases where a rolling update's 100% exposure is too risky.
Features that need real-traffic validation before full promotion.
High-stakes deployments for enterprise or regulated workloads.
GitOps workflows where automated promotion/rollback is desirable.
Simple stateless services with good staging coverage — rolling update is sufficient.
Clusters without a service mesh or Ingress controller that supports traffic splitting.
Environments without Prometheus or equivalent metrics — analysis templates have nothing to query.
Hot-fix deployments where speed matters more than gradual exposure.

section.name: manual-canary
section.p: The simplest canary uses two separate Deployments — stable and canary — that share a common Service selector label. Traffic weight is determined by replica ratio: 9 stable replicas + 1 canary replica = 10% canary traffic. This requires no special tooling but manual progression steps.

example.title: Manual canary with two Deployments
example.code: # Stable deployment (90% traffic via 9 replicas) apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: api-stable labels: app: api version: stable spec: replicas: 9 selector: matchLabels: app: api version: stable template: metadata: labels: app: api version: stable spec: containers: - name: api image: registry.example.com/api:1.0.0 --- # Canary deployment (10% traffic via 1 replica) apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: api-canary labels: app: api version: canary spec: replicas: 1 selector: matchLabels: app: api version: canary template: metadata: labels: app: api version: canary spec: containers: - name: api image: registry.example.com/api:2.0.0 --- # Service routes to both (weighted by replica count) apiVersion: v1 kind: Service metadata: name: api spec: selector: app: api # Matches both stable and canary ports: - port: 80 targetPort: 8000

section.name: nginx-ingress-canary
section.p: Nginx Ingress Controller supports canary annotations that apply traffic splitting at the Ingress level — more precise than replica weighting, supports both percentage-based and header-based routing.

example.title: Canary with Nginx Ingress weight annotation
example.code: # Stable Ingress apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1 kind: Ingress metadata: name: api-stable annotations: kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx spec: rules: - host: api.example.com http: paths: - path: / pathType: Prefix backend: service: name: api-stable port: number: 80 --- # Canary Ingress with weight apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1 kind: Ingress metadata: name: api-canary annotations: kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary: "true" nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary-weight: "10" # 10% traffic spec: rules: - host: api.example.com http: paths: - path: / pathType: Prefix backend: service: name: api-canary port: number: 80

example.title: Header-based routing for internal canary testing
example.code: apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1 kind: Ingress metadata: name: api-canary-header annotations: kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary: "true" nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary-by-header: "X-Canary" nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary-by-header-value: "true" spec: rules: - host: api.example.com http: paths: - path: / pathType: Prefix backend: service: name: api-canary port: number: 80
example.note: Header-based routing lets QA and developers test the canary version in production without affecting end users.

commands.command: kubectl delete ingress api-canary && kubectl set image deployment/api-stable api=registry.example.com/api:2.0.0

section.name: argo-rollouts
section.p: Argo Rollouts replaces a Deployment with a Rollout resource that supports step-based canary promotion, automated AnalysisTemplate queries (Prometheus, Datadog, web hooks), and automatic rollback when success conditions are not met. It is the 2025-2026 standard for enterprise production deployments and GitOps workflows.

example.title: Argo Rollouts canary with Prometheus analysis
example.note: The AnalysisTemplate queries Prometheus every minute for 5 minutes. If success rate drops below 99% three times, the rollout is automatically aborted and traffic returns to stable.

example.title: Blue-Green with Argo Rollouts
example.code:
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1 kind: Rollout metadata: name: api spec: replicas: 3 selector: matchLabels: app: api template: metadata: labels: app: api spec: containers: - name: api image: registry.example.com/api:1.0.0 ports: - containerPort: 8000
strategy: blueGreen: activeService: api-active # Production traffic previewService: api-preview # Preview/test traffic autoPromotionEnabled: false # Manual promotion required previewReplicaCount: 1 scaleDownDelaySeconds: 30

commands.command: kubectl create namespace argo-rollouts && kubectl apply -n argo-rollouts -f https://github.com/argoproj/argo-rollouts/releases/latest/download/install.yaml
kubectl argo rollouts get rollout api -w
kubectl argo rollouts retry rollout api

list.item: Error rate: less than baseline + 1%. Source: Prometheus.
P99 latency: less than baseline + 10%. Source: Prometheus.
Success rate: greater than 99%. Source: Prometheus.
Custom business metric: define per app. Source: Application.

checklist.check: Traffic splitting mechanism chosen (Ingress annotations, service mesh, or Argo Rollouts).
Metrics for analysis defined (latency, error rate, custom).
Argo Rollouts: AnalysisTemplate metric provider configured.
